clinical_history: 68 year-old man with a history of severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) with rapid heart rate. What is the rhythm? Note: Lead V2 is not recorded.
diagnosis: This ECG shows atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular response rate at about 150 bpm. Diffuse non-specific ST-T wave changes are present. The highly irregular rate with an oscillatory (wavy) baseline is typical of atrial fibrillation. There are no discrete P waves which rules out sinus or atrial tachycardia and no atrial flutter waves. There is borderline low voltage diffusely, consistent with COPD.   For reference also see cases # 310 and 332.
